TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi beforefieldinit NetNamedPipeBinding extends System.ServiceModel.Channels.Binding

Docs.summary: Provides a secure and reliable binding that is optimized for on-machine communication.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding> generates a run-time communication stack by default, which uses transport security, named pipes for message delivery, and a binary message encoding. This binding is an appropriate Windows Communication Foundation (WCF) system-provided choice for on-machine communication. It also supports transactions.
The default configuration for the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding> is similar to the configuration provided by the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetTcpBinding>, but it is simpler because the WCF implementation is only meant for on-machine use and consequently there are fewer exposed features. The most notable difference is that the <xref:System.ServiceModel.SecurityMode> setting only offers the <xref:System.ServiceModel.SecurityMode.None> and <xref:System.ServiceModel.SecurityMode.Transport> options. SOAP security support is not an included option. The security behavior is configurable using the optional `securityMode` parameter in <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding.%23ctor(System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeSecurityMode)> the constructor.
## Examples The following example shows the part of the configuration file that contains the section for the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding> with values set to defaults.
The following example shows how to programmatically use the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding> class.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_CFX/s_uenetnamedpipe/cs/service.cs" id="Snippet2":::

Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

remarks.format: ## Remarks The security behavior is configurable using the optional `securityMode` parameter in the constructor.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor() cil managed

Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The default security mode used is <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeSecurityMode.Transport>.
## Examples The following example shows the part of the configuration file that contains the section for the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding> with values set to defaults.

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(valuetype System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeSecurityMode securityMode) cil managed

Docs.param: The value that specifies whether Windows security is used with named pipes.
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a specified security mode.

remarks.format:
## Remarks Use this constructor when you want to configure the security explicitly. The default is <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeSecurityMode.Transport> when using the parameterless constructor.
## Examples :::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_CFX/s_uenetnamedpipe/cs/snippets.cs" id="Snippet3":::

MemberSignature.Value: .method public hidebysig virtual instance class System.ServiceModel.Channels.BindingElementCollection CreateBindingElements() cil managed
Public Overrides Function CreateBindingElements () As BindingElementCollection

Docs.summary: Creates a collection with the binding elements for the binding.
Docs.returns: The of type that makes up the binding.

MemberSignature.Value: Public ReadOnly Property EnvelopeVersion As EnvelopeVersion
public: property System::ServiceModel::EnvelopeVersion ^ EnvelopeVersion { System::ServiceModel::EnvelopeVersion ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the version of SOAP that is used for messages processed by this binding.

remarks.format:
## Remarks SOAP 1.2 must be used with this binding.
## Examples The following example shows how to get the version of SOAP.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_CFX/s_uenetnamedpipe/cs/service.cs" id="Snippet16":::

MemberSignature.Value: member this.MaxBufferPoolSize : int64 with get, set
public: property long MaxBufferPoolSize { long get(); void set(long value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of bytes that are used to buffer incoming messages in memory.
Docs.value: The maximum number of bytes that are used to buffer incoming messages in memory. The default value is 524,288 bytes.

remarks.format:
## Remarks If you receive more data than you can buffer, the data that exceeds the buffer size remains on the underlying socket until your buffer has room for the rest of the data.
## Examples The following example shows how to get the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding.MaxBufferPoolSize> value.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_CFX/s_uenetnamedpipe/cs/service.cs" id="Snippet5":::

MemberSignature.Value: member this.MaxBufferSize : int with get, set
public: property int MaxBufferSize { int get(); void set(int value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of bytes used to buffer incoming messages in memory.
Docs.value: The maximum number of bytes that are used to buffer incoming messages in memory. The default value is 65,536 bytes.

remarks.format:
## Remarks If you receive more data than you can buffer, the data that exceeds the buffer size remains on the underlying socket until your buffer has room for the rest of the data.
## Examples The following example shows how to get the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding.MaxBufferSize> value.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_CFX/s_uenetnamedpipe/cs/service.cs" id="Snippet6":::

Docs.exception: The value set is less than 0.

MemberSignature.Value: member this.MaxConnections : int with get, set
public: property int MaxConnections { int get(); void set(int value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of connections, both inbound and outbound, that are allowed to endpoints configured with the named pipe binding.
Docs.value: The maximum number of named pipe connections that are allowed with this binding. The default value is 10.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The maximum number of inbound and the maximum number of outbound connections are set to the same value.
## Examples The following example shows how to get the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding.MaxConnections> values.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_CFX/s_uenetnamedpipe/cs/service.cs" id="Snippet7":::

MemberSignature.Value: member this.MaxReceivedMessageSize : int64 with get, set
public: property long MaxReceivedMessageSize { long get(); void set(long value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the maximum size, in bytes, for a received message that is processed by the binding.
Docs.value: The maximum size (in bytes) for a received message that is processed by the binding. The default value is 65,536 bytes.

remarks.format:
## Examples The following example shows how to get the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding.MaxReceivedMessageSize> value.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_CFX/s_uenetnamedpipe/cs/service.cs" id="Snippet8":::

MemberSignature.Value: member this.ReaderQuotas : System.Xml.XmlDictionaryReaderQuotas with get, set
public: property System::Xml::XmlDictionaryReaderQuotas ^ ReaderQuotas { System::Xml::XmlDictionaryReaderQuotas ^ get(); void set(System::Xml::XmlDictionaryReaderQuotas ^ value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets constraints on the complexity of SOAP messages that can be processed by endpoints configured with this binding.
Docs.value: The that specifies the complexity constraints on SOAP messages exchanged. The default values for these constraints are provided in the following Remarks section.

remarks.format:
## Remarks The complexity constraints protect against a class of denial of service (DOS) attacks that attempt to use message complexity to tie up endpoint processing resources. The properties that express these constraints and their default values are as follows:
- <xref:System.Xml.XmlDictionaryReaderQuotas.MaxDepth> = 32
- <xref:System.Xml.XmlDictionaryReaderQuotas.MaxStringContentLength> = 8192
- <xref:System.Xml.XmlDictionaryReaderQuotas.MaxArrayLength> = 16384
- <xref:System.Xml.XmlDictionaryReaderQuotas.MaxBytesPerRead> = 4096
- <xref:System.Xml.XmlDictionaryReaderQuotas.MaxNameTableCharCount> = 16384
## Examples The following example shows how to get the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding.ReaderQuotas> object.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_CFX/s_uenetnamedpipe/cs/service.cs" id="Snippet11":::

MemberSignature.Value: Public Overrides ReadOnly Property Scheme As String
public: virtual property System::String ^ Scheme { System::String ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the URI transport scheme for the channels and listeners that are configured with this binding.

remarks.format:
## Examples The following example shows how to get the URI transport scheme.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_CFX/s_uenetnamedpipe/cs/service.cs" id="Snippet10":::

MemberSignature.Value: member this.Security : System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeSecurity with get, set
public: property System::ServiceModel::NetNamedPipeSecurity ^ Security { System::ServiceModel::NetNamedPipeSecurity ^ get(); void set(System::ServiceModel::NetNamedPipeSecurity ^ value); };

Docs.summary: Gets an object that specifies the type of security used with services configured with this binding.
Docs.value: The that is used with this binding. The default value is .

remarks.format:
## Remarks The <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeSecurity> provides the types of security and settings available to the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding>.
## Examples The following example shows how to get the type of security to be used with this binding.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_CFX/s_uenetnamedpipe/cs/service.cs" id="Snippet9":::

MemberSignature.Value: member this.TransferMode : System.ServiceModel.TransferMode with get, set
public: property System::ServiceModel::TransferMode TransferMode { System::ServiceModel::TransferMode get(); void set(System::ServiceModel::TransferMode value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the service configured with the binding uses streamed (in one or both directions) or buffered modes of message transfer.
Docs.value: The value that indicates whether the service configured with the binding uses streamed (in one or both directions) or buffered modes of message transfer. The default is .

remarks.format:
## Remarks It is possible to stream only the incoming or the outgoing messages by specifying, respectively, <xref:System.ServiceModel.TransferMode.StreamedRequest> or <xref:System.ServiceModel.TransferMode.StreamedResponse>.
## Examples The following example shows how to get the <xref:System.ServiceModel.NetNamedPipeBinding.TransferMode>.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/VS_Snippets_CFX/s_uenetnamedpipe/cs/service.cs" id="Snippet18":::
